name: INR/USD Exchange Rate
summary: Day 67. **INR ₹95 TRIGGER SUSTAINED ACTIVATION DAY 2** — Tue May 5 intraday ₹95.34 (+0.5% 1D fresh record); Mon May 4 closed ₹95.10 (post-Apr 30 ₹95.20 intraday low + ₹94.88 close-on-RBI-defense). The May 3 brief's question 'USD/INR holds below ₹95 sustained through Monday May 4' is RESOLVED-NO — INR did NOT hold below ₹95; broke through to fresh low. The MOST IMPORTANT non-oil deployment-signal of the crisis arc is now in CONFIRMED ACTIVATION mode rather than REVERSAL mode. Drivers: Brent re-rate +5.80% May 4 + kinetic restart + Fed-hawkish DXY firm at 98.52 + Indian capital-inflow shortfall projected $40-50B FY27. RBI intervention threshold identified at ₹95.50+ (16 paise away). Governor Malhotra's 'FX curbs won't remain forever' framing remains operative. FII flow context: April outflow ₹60,847 cr; CY26 outflow ₹1.92 trillion (already exceeds CY25 full-year ₹1.66T). India 10Y GOI eased -6 bps to 7.00% — counter-cyclical to INR (RBI absorption + foreign FAR-debt bid; NOT yet defending INR via rates). Business Standard flagging ₹96-97 next levels. Operational regime: INR-stable-stress headed toward RBI-intervention.

current.regime: ₹95 trigger ACTIVATION confirmed Day 2 sustained; INR-stable-stress regime headed toward RBI-intervention regime if ₹95.50 breached. RBI at ₹95.50 intervention threshold ~16 paise away.
